ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "year_birth" INTEGER /* Year of birth of the customer */,
  "education" TEXT /* Educational level of the customer */,
  "marital_status" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer */,
  "income" TEXT /* Annual income of the customer */,
  "kidhome" INTEGER /* Number of children in the customer's household */,
  "teenhome" INTEGER /* Number of teenagers in the customer's household */,
  "dt_customer" TEXT /* Date the customer joined the company */,
  "recency" INTEGER /* Number of days since the last purchase */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the customer is located */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the customer */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the customer */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the customer */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the customer is located */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the customer is located */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* Zip code of the customer's location */,
  "loyalty_program_status" TEXT /* Status of the customer in the loyalty program (e.g., active, inactive) */,
  "loyalty_points" INTEGER /* Number of loyalty points earned by the customer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "purchases" (
  "purchase_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each purchase */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the purchase */,
  "purchase_date" TEXT /* Date of the purchase */,
  "amount" TEXT /* Total amount spent on the purchase */,
  "product_id" INTEGER /* ID of the product purchased */,
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* ID of the purchase channel (e.g., web, store, catalog) */,
  "coupon_used" TEXT /* Coupon code used (if any) */,
  "discount_amount" TEXT /* Amount of discount applied */,
  "payment_method" TEXT /* Method of payment (e.g., credit card, cash, online banking) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("purchase_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchases_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchases_product_id FOREIGN KEY ("product_id") REFERENCES products ("product_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_purchases_channel_id FOREIGN KEY ("channel_id") REFERENCES channels ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "products" (
  "product_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the product */,
  "price" TEXT /* Price of the product */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the product */,
  "product_image_url" TEXT /* URL of the product image */,
  "stock_level" INTEGER /* Current stock level of the product */,
  "reorder_level" INTEGER /* Reorder level of the product (i.e., when to reorder stock) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "channels" (
  "channel_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each channel */,
  "channel_name" TEXT /* Name of the channel */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the channel */,
  "channel_type" TEXT /* Type of channel (e.g., online, offline, hybrid) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("channel_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaigns" (
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each campaign */,
  "campaign_name" TEXT /* Name of the campaign */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date of the campaign */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date of the campaign */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the campaign */,
  "campaign_type" TEXT /* Type of campaign (e.g., promotional, retention) */,
  "budget" TEXT /* Budget allocated for the campaign */,
  "target_audience" TEXT /* Target audience for the campaign (e.g., demographics, interests) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "campaign_responses" (
  "response_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each response */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who responded */,
  "campaign_id" INTEGER /* ID of the campaign */,
  "response_date" TEXT /* Date of the response */,
  "response_type" TEXT /* Type of response (e.g., accepted, declined) */,
  "response_channel" TEXT /* Channel through which the customer responded (e.g., email, social media) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("response_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_responses_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_campaign_responses_campaign_id FOREIGN KEY ("campaign_id") REFERENCES campaigns ("campaign_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "complaints" (
  "complaint_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each complaint */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who filed the complaint */,
  "complaint_date" TEXT /* Date the complaint was filed */,
  "complaint_details" TEXT /* Details of the complaint */,
  "resolution_date" TEXT /* Date the complaint was resolved */,
  "resolution_status" TEXT /* Status of the complaint resolution (e.g., resolved, pending) */,
  "complaint_category" TEXT /* Category of the complaint (e.g., product, service) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("complaint_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_complaints_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the platform */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date of the access */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "module_accessed" TEXT /* Module or feature accessed */,
  "action_performed" TEXT /* Action performed by the user (e.g., view customer profile, create campaign) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user (hashed for security) */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, manager, analyst) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "segmentation" (
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each segment */,
  "segment_name" TEXT /* Name of the segment */,
  "segment_description" TEXT /* Description of the segment */,
  "segment_criteria" TEXT /* Criteria used to define the segment (e.g., demographics, behavior) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("segment_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "customer_segments" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer */,
  "segment_id" INTEGER /* ID of the segment the customer belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id", "segment_id")
)
